title: Solar Execution Capsule (ECapsule) v1
description: Draft v1 schema for the Execution Capsule — a bundled dispatch unit combining Skill + MCP bindings + Policy + Contract + Verification rules. DISTINCT from the State Capsule in schemas/capsule-schema.yaml which is a sprint context transfer struct. Uses 'execution_capsule_id' (not 'capsule_id') to avoid naming collision.

execution_capsule_id.description: Unique identifier for this Execution Capsule. Prefix 'ecap-' distinguishes from State Capsule IDs. Field name 'execution_capsule_id' avoids collision with capsule-schema.yaml's 'capsule_id' if that field is ever added.

skill_id.description: Resolved Skill name from solar_skills.py registry.

node_id.description: DAG node that generated this capsule.

operator_id.description: Physical operator assigned for execution. Null until operator_runtime.py:325 submit() resolves it.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of capsule creation.

submitted_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when capsule was submitted to operator_runtime. Null until submitted.

additionalProperties.description: provider_id that was resolved for this MCP capability token.

mcp_bindings.description: Map of mcp_tool_token → resolved provider_id. Populated by APO after capability_registry.py:186 cmd_query resolution.

result.description: Policy check outcome. 'waived' requires P0_BYPASS flag.

reason.description: Explanation, especially for fail or waived.

policy_guard.description: List of policy check results. All role=enforcer skills contribute mandatory checks here.

contract.description: Machine-readable acceptance criteria. Copied from Skill's verification_contract at resolution time.

check_name.description: Name of the post-execution verification check.

verification_rules.description: Ordered list of post-execution checks. All must pass for capsule to be considered verified.

timeout_seconds.description: Hard execution timeout. operator_runtime.py enforces this at the lease level.

evidence.description: Collected evidence from verification_rules execution. Written by evidence_ledger.py.
